title: The Capture of Yazid b. al-Muhallab
description: The biographers disagree on this subject. According to Hisham b. Muhammad-Abu Mikhnaf: When Yazid b. al-Muhallab made his way to Wisil and then embarked on ships with the intention of sailing to al-Ba$rah, 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz sent 'Adi b. Artit to al-Bagah to serve as governor. 'Adi dispatched Musa b. al-Wajih al-Himyari, who arrested Yazid after overtaking him at the Bagrah bridge of the Ma'qil Canal. 284 'Adi sent Yazid to 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz in the custody of Musa b. al-Wajih. When the prisoner arrived, 'Umar b. 'Abd al-'Aziz summoned him.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 24
